target.name: (-)-Psathyrin A
target.motivation: Psathyrin A is an antibacterial diterpene natural product with a unique 6/4/5/5 tetracyclic carbon skeleton and seven contiguous stereocenters, including three adjacent all-carbon quaternary centers. The strained cyclobutane ring poses a significant synthetic challenge, and the authors are interested in leveraging MHAT-initiated radical cyclization for constructing strained ring systems.

success_criteria.free_text: First total synthesis of (-)-Psathyrin A in 19 steps.

key_disconnections.reasoning: To construct the challenging four-membered ring and establish the third all-carbon quaternary center. The C5 ketone activates the C6-C7 olefin as a radical acceptor.

alternatives_considered.strategy: Oxidative dearomatization to dienone 10a for radical cyclization
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: The C14 ketone further reacted with the newly formed dienone, complicating the reaction.

alternatives_considered.strategy: Ring contraction of a five-membered ring (e.g., Favorskii, Wolff rearrangement)
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Authors preferred MHAT-initiated radical cyclization for strained ring systems.
Authors preferred MHAT-initiated radical cyclization for strained ring systems.

key_disconnections.bond_description: Formation of 5/5-fused bicyclic ring system and all-carbon quaternary center
key_disconnections.reasoning: To form the desired five-membered ring and an all-carbon quaternary center from compound 12.

key_disconnections.bond_description: Introduction of aromatic ring as six-membered ring precursor
key_disconnections.reasoning: To assemble compound 13 from boronic acid 14 and vinyltriflate 15.

key_disconnections.bond_description: Formation of first two stereocenters and one all-carbon quaternary center
key_disconnections.named_strategy: Enantioselective copper/NHC-catalyzed conjugate addition and enolate propargylation
key_disconnections.reasoning: To derive vinyltriflate 15 from 2-methyl-2-cyclopenten-1-one (16), 1-bromo-2-butyne (18), and an isopropyl nucleophile (17).

retrosynthetic_strategy.rationale: The synthesis was designed to address the complexity of the psathyrin A skeleton, particularly the strained cyclobutane ring and multiple contiguous stereocenters. The key strategy involves a MHAT-initiated radical cyclization to form the cyclobutane, preceded by a gold(I)-catalyzed Conia-ene reaction to build the 5/5-fused bicyclic system. The initial stereocenters are set via an enantioselective conjugate addition/propargylation sequence. Peripheral decorations complete the synthesis after the core framework is established.

thought.background: The starting material is commercially available 2-methyl-2-cyclopenten-1-one (16). The goal is to set the first two stereocenters, including one all-carbon quaternary center.
thought.gap: How to achieve enantioselective formation of the first two stereocenters and an all-carbon quaternary center.
thought.decision: To achieve enantioselective formation of the first two stereocenters and an all-carbon quaternary center, a tandem copper-catalyzed enantioselective conjugate addition of an isopropyl Grignard reagent and propargylation with 1-bromo-2-butyne (18) was chosen, using chiral N-heterocyclic carbene (NHC) ligand 19, expecting product 20 in high yield and enantiomeric excess.

reaction.named_reaction: Copper-catalyzed enantioselective conjugate addition and propargylation

outcome.observation: Product 20 was obtained in 87% yield and 81% ee on a multigram scale.

evidence.text_span: Our synthesis started with 16 being converted to 20 via a tandem copper-catalyzed enantioselective conjugate addition of an isopropyl Grignard reagent and propargylation with 18 (Scheme 1). The enantiomer of 20 was prepared in our peyssonnoside A total synthesis.4 In the current case, the use of chiral N-heterocyclic carbene (NHC) ligand 19 gave product 20 in 87% yield and 81% ee on multigram scale.
evidence.section: Total Synthesis of (−)-Psathyrin A Enabled by Radical Cyclization

thought.gap: How to convert the ketone 20 into a vinyl triflate 15 for subsequent Suzuki-Miyaura coupling.
thought.decision: To convert 20 to vinyltriflate 15, the Comins' protocol was chosen, expecting good yield.

evidence.text_span: After 20 was converted to vinyltriflate 15 using the Comins’ protocol,11 a Suzuki−Miyaura cross coupling reaction12 between 15 and commercially available (4-methoxyphenyl)boronic acid (21) gave 22 in good yield.
evidence.section: Total Synthesis of (−)-Psathyrin A Enabled by Radical Cyclization

thought.gap: How to introduce an aromatic ring as the six-membered ring precursor.
thought.decision: To introduce an aromatic ring, a Suzuki-Miyaura cross coupling reaction between 15 and commercially available (4-methoxyphenyl)boronic acid (21) was chosen, expecting good yield of 22.

outcome.observation: Product 22 was obtained in good yield.

evidence.text_span: After 20 was converted to vinyltriflate 15 using the Comins’ protocol,11 a Suzuki−Miyaura cross coupling reaction12 between 15 and commercially available (4-methoxyphenyl)boronic acid (21) gave 22 in good yield.
evidence.section: Total Synthesis of (−)-Psathyrin A Enabled by Radical Cyclization

thought.gap: How to prepare for the gold(I)-catalyzed Conia-ene reaction to form the 5/5-fused bicyclic ring system.
thought.decision: To prepare for the Conia-ene reaction, electrophilic epoxidation of 22 with m-CPBA was chosen, expecting epoxide 23.

outcome.observation: Epoxide 23 was formed, but it started to undergo Meinwald rearrangement during NMR analysis.

evidence.text_span: Electrophilic epoxidation with m-CPBA gave epoxide 23. Interestingly, 23 started to undergo a Meinwald rearrangement13 to afford ketone 24 in deuterated chloroform during NMR analysis.
evidence.section: Total Synthesis of (−)-Psathyrin A Enabled by Radical Cyclization

thought.background: Epoxide 23 was formed and started to rearrange to ketone 24.
thought.gap: How to accelerate the Meinwald rearrangement to obtain ketone 24 efficiently.
thought.decision: To accelerate the rearrangement, p-TsOH was added to the reaction mixture, expecting to obtain ketone 24 in good yield.

outcome.observation: Ketone 24 was obtained in 87% yield from 22.

evidence.text_span: To further accelerate the rearrangement process, p-TsOH was added to the same reaction pot to provide ketone 24 in 87% yield from 22.
evidence.section: Total Synthesis of (−)-Psathyrin A Enabled by Radical Cyclization

thought.background: Ketone 24 is ready for the Conia-ene reaction.
thought.gap: How to construct the 5/5-fused bicyclic ring system and the second all-carbon quaternary center.
thought.decision: To construct the 5/5-fused bicyclic ring system and the second all-carbon quaternary center, a gold(I)-catalyzed Conia-ene reaction of 24 with a combination of AuCl(PPh3) and AgOTf in toluene was chosen, expecting bicyclic product 25.

outcome.observation: Bicyclic product 25 with vicinal all-carbon quaternary centers at its ring junction was produced in 81% yield.

evidence.text_span: Upon treatment of 24 with a combination of AuCl(PPh3) and AgOTf in toluene, bicyclic product 25 with vicinal all-carbon quaternary centers at its ring junction was produced in 81% yield to set up the stage for the proposed MHAT-initiated radical cyclization to form the four-membered ring and the third all-carbon quaternary center.
evidence.section: Total Synthesis of (−)-Psathyrin A Enabled by Radical Cyclization

thought.background: Bicyclic product 25 is ready for the proposed MHAT-initiated radical cyclization. The initial plan was to prepare dienone 10a via oxidative dearomatization.
thought.gap: How to prepare dienone 10a from 25 via oxidative dearomatization.
thought.decision: To prepare dienone 10a, oxidative dearomatization protocols were attempted on 25.

outcome.observation: Unable to prepare substrates such as 10a via oxidative dearomatization protocols because the C14 ketone further reacted with the newly formed dienone to complicate the reaction.

evidence.text_span: Unfortunately, we were unable to prepare substrates such as 10a via oxidative dearomatization protocols because the C14 ketone further reacted with the newly formed dienone to complicate the reaction.
evidence.section: Total Synthesis of (−)-Psathyrin A Enabled by Radical Cyclization

thought.background: Oxidative dearomatization to 10a failed. The next step is the radical cyclization.
thought.gap: How to perform the radical cyclization on substrate 25.
thought.decision: Radical cyclization attempts were made with substrate 25.

outcome.observation: Radical cyclization attempts with substrate 25 were unsuccessful.

evidence.text_span: Our radical cyclization attempts with substrate 25 were unsuccessful as well.
evidence.section: Total Synthesis of (−)-Psathyrin A Enabled by Radical Cyclization

thought.background: Attempts to prepare dienone 10a and perform radical cyclization on 25 failed.
thought.gap: A new strategy is needed to prepare a suitable substrate for the MHAT-initiated radical cyclization.
thought.decision: The strategy was switched to preparing enone substrate 27, which involves reducing the ketone, Birch reduction, hydrolysis, acetate protection, and double bond isomerization.

evidence.text_span: We then switched to preparing enone substrate 27.
evidence.section: Total Synthesis of (−)-Psathyrin A Enabled by Radical Cyclization

thought.background: The strategy is to prepare enone substrate 27 from 25.
thought.gap: How to reduce the ketone, perform Birch reduction and hydrolysis, and protect the secondary alcohol.
thought.decision: To prepare 26, the ketone functionality of 25 was first reduced with LiAlH4, followed by one-pot Birch reduction and hydrolysis, and then acetate protection of the secondary alcohol, expecting smooth conversion.

reaction.observation: The ketone functionality of 25 was first reduced to a secondary alcohol with LiAlH4. The subsequent one-pot Birch reduction and hydrolysis, followed by acetate protection of the secondary alcohol, gave 26 smoothly.

outcome.observation: Product 26 was obtained smoothly with an overall yield of 97% for the three steps.

evidence.text_span: The ketone functionality of 25 was first reduced to a secondary alcohol with LiAlH4. The subsequent one-pot Birch reduction and hydrolysis, followed by acetate protection of the secondary alcohol, gave 26 smoothly.
evidence.section: Total Synthesis of (−)-Psathyrin A Enabled by Radical Cyclization

thought.background: Compound 26 has been synthesized. The next step is double bond isomerization to form enone 27.
thought.gap: How to achieve the double bond isomerization with good conversion and diastereoselectivity.
thought.decision: Isomerization of 26 was attempted with HCl (1.0 M in Et2O) in THF at 0 °C for 6 h.

outcome.observation: The isomerization had low conversion (~30%) and the diastereoselectivity was sensitive to reaction conditions. The recovered starting material was subjected to the same conditions again.

evidence.text_span: The next double bond isomerization turned out to be nontrivial. The isomerization has low conversion and the diastereoselectivity is sensitive to the reaction time, temperature, and scale. When 1.79 g of 26 was treated with HCl (1.0 M in Et2O) in THF at 0 °C for 6 h, ~30% conversion was achieved.14 The recovered starting material was subjected to the same reaction conditions again.
evidence.section: Total Synthesis of (−)-Psathyrin A Enabled by Radical Cyclization

thought.background: The initial attempt at double bond isomerization had low conversion.
thought.gap: How to improve the conversion and obtain product 27 with desired stereochemistry.
thought.decision: The recovered starting material 26 was subjected to the same reaction conditions (HCl (1.0 M in Et2O) in THF at 0 °C for 6 h) for a second cycle to improve conversion and avoid significant loss of starting material.

outcome.observation: After two cycles, product 27 was produced in 61% yield (dr = 8.5/1) with the rest of 26 recovered. The major diastereomer had the desired stereochemistry at C2.

evidence.text_span: After two cycles, product 27 was produced in 61% yield (dr = 8.5/1) with the rest of 26 recovered to avoid significant loss of the starting material. To our delight, the major diastereomer has the desired stereochemistry at C2.
evidence.section: Total Synthesis of (−)-Psathyrin A Enabled by Radical Cyclization

thought.background: Enone substrate 27 has been successfully prepared with the desired stereochemistry.
thought.gap: How to construct the four-membered ring and establish the third all-carbon quaternary center using MHAT-initiated radical cyclization.
thought.decision: To construct the four-membered ring and establish the third all-carbon quaternary center, the Baran reductive olefin coupling conditions (Fe(acac)3, PhSiH3, EtOH) were chosen for the MHAT-initiated radical cyclization of 27, expecting excellent yield of 28.

reaction.named_reaction: Baran reductive olefin coupling (MHAT-initiated radical cyclization)

outcome.observation: Compound 27 underwent MHAT-initiated radical cyclization to provide 28 in excellent yield, showcasing the efficiency of such MHAT chemistry in building strained ring systems.

evidence.text_span: With the Baran reductive olefin coupling conditions (Fe-(acac) , PhSiH , EtOH),7a compound 27 underwent MHATinitiated radical cyclization to provide 28 in excellent yield, showcasing the efficiency of such MHAT chemistry in building strained ring systems!
evidence.section: Total Synthesis of (−)-Psathyrin A Enabled by Radical Cyclization

thought.background: Tetracyclic product 28 is in hand. The next stage is peripheral decorations.
thought.gap: How to introduce a hydroxyl group at C6.
thought.decision: To introduce a hydroxyl group at C6, a one-pot Rubottom oxidation was chosen, involving selective silyl enol ether formation and dihydroxylation, expecting to give 29.

outcome.observation: Product 29 was obtained via a one-pot Rubottom oxidation.

evidence.text_span: With tetracyclic product 28 in hand, we next focused on peripheral decorations to complete the total synthesis of (−)-psathyrin A. In addition to facilitating the MHAT-initiated radical cyclization, the C5 ketone functionality allowed introduction of a hydroxyl group at C6 via a one-pot Rubottom oxidation,15 namely, selective silyl enol ether formation and dihydroxylation, to give 29.
evidence.section: Total Synthesis of (−)-Psathyrin A Enabled by Radical Cyclization

thought.background: Compound 29 has been synthesized. The secondary alcohol needs protection, and a methyl carboxylate group needs to be introduced at C4.
thought.gap: How to protect the secondary alcohol and introduce the carboxylate group, followed by conversion to an olefin.
thought.decision: To protect the secondary alcohol, it was protected as a TBS ether. Then, a methyl carboxylate group was introduced at C4 using Mander's protocol, followed by a one-pot α-selenation and oxidative syn-elimination to afford compound 30.

outcome.observation: Compound 30 was obtained in good yield after TBS protection, Mander's protocol, and α-selenation/oxidative syn-elimination.

evidence.text_span: The resulting secondary alcohol was protected as a TBS ether. The C5 ketone further enabled us to introduce a methyl carboxylate group at C4 using Mander’s protocol.16 The carboxylate group served as a precursor of the hydroxymethyl group encoded by the target molecule. A subsequent one-pot α-selenation and oxidative syn-elimination afforded compound 30 in good yield.
evidence.section: Total Synthesis of (−)-Psathyrin A Enabled by Radical Cyclization

thought.background: Compound 30 has been synthesized. The C5 ketone needs to be reduced to a methylene group.
thought.gap: How to reduce the C5 ketone to a methylene group without affecting the strained cyclobutane ring.
thought.decision: To reduce the C5 ketone to a methylene group, a sequence of Luche reduction and Barton-McCombie deoxygenation was chosen, expecting the strained cyclobutane ring to survive.

outcome.observation: Compound 31 was obtained in 68% yield. The strained cyclobutane ring survived this radical deoxygenation process.

evidence.text_span: At this stage, it was time to reduce the C5 ketone to a methylene group and convert 30 to 31. This task was achieved by a sequence of Luche reduction17 and Barton-McCombie deoxygenation.18 Notably, the strained cyclobutane ring survived this radical deoxygenation process.
evidence.section: Total Synthesis of (−)-Psathyrin A Enabled by Radical Cyclization

thought.background: Compound 31 has been synthesized. The carboxylate and acetate groups need to be reduced, and the primary alcohol selectively protected.
thought.gap: How to reduce the carboxylate and acetate groups and selectively protect the primary alcohol.
thought.decision: To reduce the carboxylate group and acetate group, DIBAL-H was used. The resulting primary alcohol was then selectively protected as a TBS ether to give 32.

evidence.text_span: The carboxylate group and acetate group of 31 were then reduced with DIBAL-H. The resulting primary alcohol was selectively protected as a TBS ether to give 32 in 94% yield.
evidence.section: Total Synthesis of (−)-Psathyrin A Enabled by Radical Cyclization

thought.background: Compound 32 has been synthesized. The final steps are to oxidize the secondary alcohol and remove the protecting groups to obtain (-)-Psathyrin A.
thought.gap: How to oxidize the secondary alcohol at C4 and remove the two TBS protecting groups.
thought.decision: To complete the total synthesis, IBX oxidation of the secondary alcohol at C4 was performed, followed by removal of the two TBS protecting groups with HF/pyridine.

outcome.observation: The total synthesis of (-)-Psathyrin A was completed in 19 steps.

evidence.text_span: IBX oxidation of the secondary alcohol at C4 followed by removal of the two TBS protecting groups with HF/pyridine completed the total synthesis of (−)-psathyrin A in 19 steps.
evidence.section: Total Synthesis of (−)-Psathyrin A Enabled by Radical Cyclization

Overall Yield.interpretation: Overall yield not explicitly stated, but individual step yields are provided.

Failed Attempts Count.interpretation: Number of explicitly mentioned failed attempts.

validation.significance: This is the first enantioselective total synthesis of (-)-Psathyrin A, highlighting the power of MHAT-initiated radical cyclization for constructing highly congested and strained cyclobutane rings within complex natural product frameworks. It also showcases efficient strategies for setting multiple contiguous stereocenters and all-carbon quaternary centers.

source.title: Total Synthesis of (-)-Psathyrin A Enabled by Radical Cyclization.
source.authors: Zhao W, Al-Ahmad R, Dai M.
